title: Brazil 3-0 Scotland settles the BTTS ticket as a loss
summary: Scotland failed to score in a 3-0 Brazil win, so the fake BTTS Yes leg is settled as lost and recovery mode reduces future stake size.

title: Morocco 4-2 Haiti kills the under-total ticket
summary: Six total goals landed well above the under 2.5 line, so the old totals pick and the related two-leg multi are now shown as settled losses.

title: England and Ghana enter pivotal Group L window
summary: Group L scenarios keep England, Ghana, Croatia, and Panama live for multiple Round of 32 paths. The model flags team news and final-match motivation as high-impact inputs.

title: Argentina form signal strengthens after Austria result
summary: Argentina's tournament form remains a positive model input, increasing confidence in short-price projections while reducing value at common market prices.

title: Portugal draw adds caution to favorite pricing
summary: A favorite failing to convert dominance into margin is treated as a caution flag for spread-style markets, not a full downgrade to baseline strength.

title: Late comeback reinforces volatility in elimination-pressure matches
summary: Comeback-heavy group fixtures increase model caution for live-betting assumptions and support keeping draw and totals uncertainty visible.

title: Bankroll limits and no-chasing rules are part of the model context
summary: The fake bankroll challenge uses spending limits, time limits, and no-chasing rules as hard constraints. This is simulated analytics, not real-money betting advice.
